frameworks.name: multicomponent working memory with distributed executive control
frameworks.one_line: WM as four modules — two domain-general capacity-limited, two domain-specific time-limited

frameworks.name: episodic buffer model
frameworks.one_line: multimodal store consolidating into episodic long-term memory via attentional refreshment

frameworks.name: phonological loop / visuospatial sketchpad
frameworks.one_line: domain-specific time-limited modules maintained by inner speech and image revival

core_question: How can a multicomponent working memory architecture with distributed executive control account for interactions between WM and multi-tasking performance?
key_assumptions: executive control is distributed rather than housed in a single central executive
domain-general modules are capacity-limited; domain-specific modules are time-limited
WM operates by interfacing with declarative and procedural long-term memory, sensory registers, and motor output
key_findings: episodic buffer stores multimodal representations and uses attentional refreshment against decay
executive module maintains domain-general task-relevant information
phonological buffer maintains verbal content via inner-speech rehearsal
visuospatial module holds visual/spatial representations via image revival
interpretations: distributed executive control across modules accounts for WM and multi-tasking interactions
WM functions via coupling with LTM, sensory input, and motor output rather than as a standalone store
